fabricated_divergences.quote: German outlets (index 1, 2) center Chancellor Merz's defensive pushback against Trump's 'ridiculous' spending criticism, while the Turkish Anadolu piece (index 9) presents the US demand for 5% GDP spending through Ambassador Whitaker's framing that ranks Germany among the leaders rather than laggards.
fabricated_divergences.why_unsupported: Article_metadata lists index 2 as language 'es', country 'Spain', outlet 'El Pais' - it is the Spanish-language article, not a German outlet.

fabricated_divergences.quote: Ukrainian media (Kyiv Independent, Politico Europe articles sourced from Ukraine) foreground the diplomatic crisis between Poland and Ukraine, including historical grievances and its impact on aid pledges, whereas other regional clusters treat the Poland-Ukraine friction as peripheral or omit it entirely.
fabricated_divergences.why_unsupported: Article_metadata lists both Politico Europe articles (index 6, index 11) as country 'Belgium', not Ukraine.

fabricated_divergences.quote: German-language coverage (Tagesschau) presents the aid pledge as a straightforward factual commitment replacing US funding, while English-language UK and US-oriented sources (The Guardian, Anadolu Agency) frame the same pledge as largely symbolic and primarily aimed at managing Trump's dissatisfaction with the alliance.
fabricated_divergences.why_unsupported: The 'largely symbolic' aid-pledge framing appears in index 4 (Japan Today), not in The Guardian (index 3, Trump/Iran) or Anadolu (index 9, 5% spending).

fabricated_divergences.quote: German-language coverage (Tagesschau) characterizes the Ukraine aid pledge as effectively replacing halted US funding, whereas English-language coverage from Turkey (Anadolu) and the UK (Guardian) frames the same pledge primarily as a symbolic gesture meant to reassure Trump that Europe has taken over financing.
fabricated_divergences.why_unsupported: The 'symbolic gesture' framing is index 4 (Japan Today), not Anadolu or Guardian.

fabricated_divergences.quote: Ukrainian-outlet coverage (Kyiv Independent) frames the Poland-Ukraine relationship as a bilateral diplomatic crisis, while Turkish-outlet (Anadolu) and Belgian-outlet (Politico) coverage of the same summit largely omit this friction and instead emphasize burden-sharing and unity themes.
fabricated_divergences.why_unsupported: Index 11 (Politico Europe, Belgium) is centrally about the Poland-Ukraine dispute, contradicting the claim that Belgian/Politico coverage omits it.
